prompt: Set up a StudioOps Offline Photo & Video Service Platform that enables photography and videography teams to manage service offerings, client access, and performance insights entirely on a local network. The Angular web app provides a role-based experience for Guest, Client, Merchant (studio staff), Operations, and Administrator users, with English UI and responsive layouts for desktop and tablet. Merchants configure service packages such as “Wedding Essentials” ($2,500.00, 6 hours) and “Corporate Headshots” ($450.00, 90 minutes) with optional add-ons like extra retouched images ($15.00 each) or second shooter ($80.00/hour), while controlling visibility as public, private, or restricted to specific clients. Merchants curate portfolios by category and tag (e.g., “Lifestyle,” “Product,” “Black & White”), upload photo/video assets, and receive immediate in-app feedback on file acceptance, automatic local compression targets (photos downscaled to a max 3000px long edge, JPEG quality 80; videos capped to 1080p where feasible), and preview generation. Operations users review dashboards that visualize event/job popularity, registration-to-confirmation conversion, attendance/arrival rate, cancellation rate, and channel/tag distribution, with filters by date range (MM/DD/YYYY), organization, and event/job type; exports must produce offline-ready CSV and Excel files saved to a user-selected local folder. The backend uses Fastify to expose REST-style APIs consumed by the Angular client, enforcing authentication, authorization, validation, and consistent error handling without any internet dependency. PostgreSQL persists users, roles, permissions, organizations, service offerings, portfolio metadata, analytics snapshots, audit logs, and rules. Authentication is strictly local username + password; passwords must be at least 12 characters with complexity rules, stored using Argon2id, and accounts lock for 15 minutes after 5 failed attempts in 10 minutes. Conflicting upstream items such as third-party login, SSO, SMS/email verification, mobile login, and real-name verification are not implemented. RBAC must support resource-level controls (e.g., a Merchant can edit only their own offerings; Operations can view analytics across assigned orgs; Admin can manage roles), with sensitive configurations (stored proxy settings or service accounts used for internal integrations) encrypted at rest using AES-256-GCM and masked in UI. All read/write actions on protected resources generate immutable access audit logs retained for 365 days. API authentication uses signed JWTs issued by Fastify with a 30-minute access lifetime and 8-hour sliding session limit; rate limiting defaults to 120 requests/minute per user and 30/minute for Guests. A rules engine enforces configurable quotas and penalties such as max 20 asset uploads/day per Merchant, 10 portfolio edits/hour, 60-second cooldown between export requests, priority bypass for whitelisted users, and escalating penalties (temporary feature disablement for 30 minutes after 3 violations). Rules are versioned, support effective dates, and allow canary rollout to 10% of targeted users. Data cleansing runs on import/upload metadata and internal feeds: it normalizes currency to USD, durations to minutes, and dimensions to inches, flags missing/outlier values for review, and deduplicates near-identical listings using multi-feature similarity on title, duration, price, and tags with merge workflows that preserve provenance and require an Operations or Admin approval.
